etymology.description: semantic 心(心) (xīn, "heart") + phonetic 壹(壹) (yī) -> symbolizes virtuous character and qualities
etymology.interpretation: The character 懿(yì, "virtuous") combines 心(心) (xīn, "heart") as the semantic component, suggesting inner qualities or virtue, with 壹(壹) (yī) as the phonetic component, contributing the sound. The use of a complex component historically associated with offerings emphasizes reverence and moral excellence, reinforcing the meaning of 'virtuous'.
